questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
A hydrogen atom changes its state from $n=3$ to $n=2$. Due to recoil, the percentage change in the wave length of emitted light is approximately $1 \times 10^{-n}$. The value of $n$ is_____. 
[Given $\mathrm{Rhc}=13.6 \mathrm{eV}, \mathrm{hc}=1242 \mathrm{eV} \mathrm{nm}, \mathrm{h}=6.6 \times 10^{-34} \mathrm{~J} \mathrm{~s}$ mass of the hydrogenatom $=1.6 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~kg}$ ]
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Hydrogen atom is bombarded with electrons accelerated through a potential different of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>V</mi></math>, which causes excitation of hydrogen atoms. If the experiment is being formed at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>. The minimum potential difference needed to observe any Balmer series lines in the emission spectra will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>α</mi><mn>10</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo></math>_________.(Write the value to the nearest integer)
questions.solution:
For minimum potential difference, electron has to make transition from n=3 to n=2  state but first electron has to reach to n=3 state from ground state. So, energy of bombarding electron should be equal to energy difference of n=3 and n=1 state.

ΔE=13.61-132e=eV

⇒13.6×89=V

⇒V=12.09 V≈12.1 V

So, α=121.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A particular hydrogen - like ion emits the radiation of frequency <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>15</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math> when it makes transition from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> The frequency of radiation emitted in transition from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>=3</mo></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>9</mn></mfrac><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>15</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi><mo>,</mo></math> when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _____.
questions.solution:
For Hydrogen like atom, energy emitted is E=-13.6Z21ni2-1nf2

⇒E=C1nf2-1ni2

⇒hν=C1nf2-1ni2

Therefore, the  ratiov1v2=1nf2-1ni22-11nf2-1ni23-1

=11-1411-19=3489

=34×98

⇒v1v2=2732

⇒v2=3227v1=3227×3×1015 Hz=329×1015 Hz

Hence, x=32.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
In an alpha particle scattering experiment distance of closest approach for the $\alpha$ particle is $4.5 \times 10^{-14} \mathrm{~m}$. If target nucleus has atomic number 80 , then maximum velocity of $\alpha$ - particle is _______ $\times 10^5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ approximately.
$\left(\frac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_0}=9 \times 10^9\right.$ SI unit, mass of $\alpha$ particle $\left.=6.72 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~kg}\right)$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Which figure shows the correct variation of applied potential difference (V) with photoelectric current (I) at two different intensities of light $\left(\mathrm{I}_1 < \mathrm{I}_2\right)$ of same wavelengths :
questions.solution:
Given lights are of same wavelength.
Hence stopping potential will remain same.
Since $I_2>I_1$, hence saturation current corresponding to $\mathrm{I}_2$ will be greater than that corresponding to $\mathrm{I}_1$.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Given below are two statements : Statement I : Figure shows the variation of stopping potential with frequency $(v)$ for the two photosensitive materials $\mathrm{M}_1$ and $\mathrm{M}_2$. The slope gives value of $\frac{\mathrm{h}}{\mathrm{e}}$, where $\mathrm{h}$ is Planck's constant, $\mathrm{e}$ is the charge of electron. Statement II : $\mathrm{M}_2$ will emit photoelectrons of greater kinetic energy for the incident radiation having same frequency. In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct and Statement II is incorrect
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.solution:
(Theory)
Photoelectric effect prove particle nature of light.

questions.q: Average force exerted on a non-reflecting surface at normal incidence is $2.4 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~N}$. If $360 \mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{cm}^2$ is the light energy flux during span of 1 hour 30 minutes, Then the area of the surface is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The de-Broglie wavelength of an electron is the same as that of a photon. If velocity of electron is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>%</mo></math> of the velocity of light, then the ratio of K.E. of electron and K.E. of photon will be:
questions.solution:
For photon,

EP=hcλp⇒λp=hcEP

For electron

λe=hmeve=hve2 Ke

Given ve=0.25c. So,

λe=h×0.25c2 Ke=hc8 Ke

Also λp=λe. Thus,

hcEp=hc8Ke⇒KeEp=18
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: When a metal surface is illuminated by light of wavelength <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi></math>, the stopping potential is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. When the same surface is illuminated by light of wavelength <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mi>λ</mi></math>, stopping potential is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></math>. The threshold wavelength for this surface is :
questions.solution:
From photoelectric effect equation, we can write E=ϕ+Kmax

and work function can be written as ϕ=hcλ0.

Also, maximum kinetic energy Kmax=eV0

For first case:

8e=hcλ-hcλ0   ... (i)

For second case:

2e=hc3λ-hcλ0    ... (ii)

After using equation (ii) in (i), we get

4hc3λ-hcλ0=hcλ-hcλ0

⇒λ0=9λ
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In a photoelectric effect experiment a light of frequency <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> times the threshold frequency is made to fall on the surface of photosensitive material. Now if the frequency is halved and intensity is doubled, the number of photo electrons emitted will be:
questions.solution:
Given, fi=f0. Now, f=fi2=f02.

Since f02<f0 i.e. the incident frequency is less than threshold frequency. Hence, there will be no emission of photoelectrons.

Therefore, current =0 and no photo electrons will be emitted.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
A star has $100 \%$ helium composition. It starts to convert three ${ }^4 \mathrm{He}$ into one ${ }^{12} \mathrm{C}$ via triple alpha process as ${ }^4 \mathrm{He}+{ }^4 \mathrm{He}+{ }^4 \mathrm{He} \rightarrow{ }^{12} \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{Q}$. The mass of the star is $2.0 \times 10^{32} \mathrm{~kg}$ and it generates energy at the rate of $5.808 \times 10^{30} \mathrm{~W}$. The rate of converting these ${ }^4 \mathrm{He}$ to ${ }^{12} \mathrm{C}$ is $\mathrm{n} \times 10^{42} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$, where $\mathrm{n}$ is _________
[ Take, mass of ${ }^4 \mathrm{He}=4.0026 \mathrm{u}$, mass of ${ }^{12} \mathrm{C}=12 \mathrm{u}$ ]
questions.solution:
${ }^4 \mathrm{He}+{ }^4 \mathrm{He}+{ }^4 \mathrm{He} \rightarrow{ }^{12} \mathrm{C}+\mathrm{Q}$
power generated $=\frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{Q}$
where, $\mathrm{N} \rightarrow$ No. of reaction $/ \mathrm{sec}$.
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Q}=\left(3 \mathrm{~m}_{\mathrm{He}}-\mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{C}}\right) \mathrm{C}^2 \\ & \mathrm{Q}=(3 \times 4.0026-12)\left(3 \times 10^8\right)^2 \\ & \mathrm{Q}=7.266 \mathrm{MeV}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{t}}=\frac{\text { power }}{\mathrm{Q}}=\frac{5.808 \times 10^{30}}{7.266 \times 10^6 \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19}} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{t}}=5 \times 10^{42}\end{aligned}$
rate of conversion of ${ }^4 \mathrm{He}$ into ${ }^{12} \mathrm{C}=15 \times 10^{42}$ Hence, $\mathrm{n}=15$
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The energy released in the fusion of $2 \mathrm{~kg}$ of hydrogen deep in the sun is $E_H$ and the energy released in the fission of $2 \mathrm{~kg}$ of ${ }^{235} \mathrm{U}$ is $E_U$. The ratio $\frac{E_H}{E_U}$ is approximately:
(Consider the fusion reaction as $4 \mid H+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow{ }_2^4 \mathrm{He}+2 v+6 \gamma+26.7 \mathrm{MeV}$, energy released in the fission reaction of ${ }^{235} \mathrm{U}$ is $200 \mathrm{MeV}$ per fission nucleus and $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=$ $\left.6.023 \times 10^{23}\right)$
questions.solution:
In each fusion reaction, $4{ }_1^1 \mathrm{H}$ nucleus are used. 
Energy released per Nuclei of ${ }_1^1 \mathrm{H}=\frac{26.7}{4} \mathrm{MeV}$
$\therefore$ Energy released by $2 \mathrm{~kg}$ hydrogen $\left(\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{H}}\right)$
$=\frac{2000}{1} \times \mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}} \times \frac{26.7}{4} \mathrm{MeV}$
$\qquad \qquad\quad \&$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \text { Energy released by } 2 \mathrm{~kg} \text { Vranium }\left(\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{V}}\right) \\ & =\frac{2000}{235} \times \mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}} \times 200 \mathrm{MeV}\end{aligned}$
So,
$\frac{E_H}{E_V}=235 \times \frac{26.7}{4 \times 200}=7.84$
$\therefore$ Approximately close to 7.62
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
From the statements given below :

(A) The angular momentum of an electron in nth orbit is an integral multiple of h.

(B) Nuclear forces do not obey inverse square law.

(C) Nuclear forces are spin dependent.

(D) Nuclear forces are central and charge independent.

(E) Stability of nucleus is inversely proportional to the value of packing fraction.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
(A) Electron revolves around nucleus only in those orbits for which the angular momentum is an integral multiple of h2π.

(B) Nuclear force does not follow the inverse square law.

(C) Nuclear forces are spin dependent but charge independent.

(D) Nuclear force is attractive in nature. It is a non central force, meaning it does not depend upon the distance between the particles but rather upon their orientation. Nuclear force is stronger if the spin is parallel; and weak if it is anti parallel.

(E) The negative value (less quantity) of packing fraction indicates that the isotope is stable. Positive value of packing fraction implies unstable (radioactive) nucleus. Also, the stability of the nucleus is inversely proportional to the value of the packing fraction.
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The explosive in a Hydrogen bomb is a mixture of H21,H31 and Li63 in some condensed form. The chain reaction is given by Li63+n10→He42+H31;  H21+H31→He42+n10

During the explosion the energy released is approximately [Given : M(Li)=6.01690 amu, MH21=2.01471 amu, MHe42=4.00388 amu and 1 amu=931.5 MeV]
questions.solution:
Combining the reactions, it can be written that

Li63+n10→He42+H31

H21+H31→He42+n10

_____________________Li63+H21→2He42

Hence, the energy released in process can be calculated as follows

Q=Δmc2=M(Li)+MH21-2×MHe42×931.5 MeV=[6.01690+2.01471-2×4.00388]×931.5 MeV=22.22 MeV
questions.source: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
